source: Singapore, Penang and Malacca Municipalities management of funds__CO273:669:5
filename: Singapore, Penang and Malacca Municipalities management of funds__CO273:669:5.txt
summary:
- **Core topic/purpose:** Wartime arrangements for administering and safeguarding the **London-held funds and securities** of the **Singapore, Penang (Georgetown), and Malacca municipalities**, so that obligations (especially **loan interest**) could continue to be paid despite the municipalities being unable to function.
- **Geography/time period:** Concerns **Malayan municipalities (Straits Settlements)** with financial administration in the **United Kingdom (London)** during **1942–1943** (Japanese occupation context implied); file notes “Closed until 1972.”
- **Major decisions/proposals:** Decision that the **Crown Agents for the Colonies** should **take over custody/management** of municipal monies and securities previously held by banks (notably the **Chartered Bank of India, Australia & China**; also **Hong Kong & Shanghai Banking Corporation**), because existing agents (**Messrs. Peirce & Williams**) had **limited authority** and banks demanded clearer legal cover.
- **Indemnities and approvals:** Banks agreed to transfer/control only if given an **indemnity**; the **Colonial Office** sought and obtained **Treasury concurrence** for indemnities to be issued **“on behalf of H.M.G.”** (not merely in the name of the Straits Settlements Government/Secretary of State). Similar indemnities were extended to other institutions involved in registrations/transfers (e.g., **Midland Bank**; **Bank of England**).
- **Key actors/organizations:** Colonial Office officials including **G.E.J. Gent**, **J.J. Paskin**, **E. (Edwin) Taylor**, **B. Foster-Hall**, **Sir Sidney Abrahams**; **Crown Agents** representatives including **H.F. Downie** and **J. Goldburg**; **Peirce & Williams** (consulting engineers/agents); banks: **Chartered Bank**, **Hong Kong & Shanghai Banking Corporation**, **Midland Bank**, **Bank of England**; **Inland Revenue**; **Treasury** (incl. **G.H.B. Chance**).
- **Obligations/events prompting action:** Need to make payments for **interest on municipal dollar loans** (converted to **sterling interest payments** per H.M.G. decision), plus **commercial debts** and **payments to dependants of interned employees**; discussions and meetings held late **Dec 1942–Jan 1943** to finalize transfer and documentation.
- **Notable numbers/dates:** Transfers targeted **as at close of business 31 Dec 1942** (Singapore & Penang) and **31 Jan 1943** (Malacca securities). Specific holdings mentioned include **£30,000 3½% War Loan (1952 or after)** in the name of **Municipal Commissioners of Georgetown, Penang** (later transferred to Crown Agents), and **£122,584 Town of Singapore 4% Sterling Debenture Stock, 1913** (registrar: Midland Bank). Balance figures (as at **21 Dec 1941**) include **Singapore deposit at call £265,000** and various smaller account balances.
- **Income tax issue (resolved negatively):** Crown Agents explored whether municipal funds could be treated as **“Colonial Government funds”** (potentially enabling **UK income tax exemption** / Joint Colonial Fund deposit), but **Inland Revenue (1 Apr 1943)** ruled the **exemption does not extend to municipalities**, and this was accepted.
